data.title: Lululemon CEO sells $160M of Amer Sports stock
data.description:
Shares of Amer Sports (NYSE:AS) are under pressure in early Friday trading after a regulatory filing disclosed a large block of shares were sold by Lululemon (LULU) founder Chip Wilson.

Wilson sold ~$4M shares in the sports equipment maker for a total value of just under $160M. Following the sale, he retains an 18% stake in Amer Sports (NYSE:AS).

While the sale was reportedly unrelated to Wilson’s confidence in Amer Sports’ (NYSE:AS) performance and instead appears to be an effort to raise cash, the news weighed on shares of Amer Sports (AS), driving the stock down nearly 2% in premarket trading.

According to Bloomberg, Wilson’s transaction occurs just weeks after pledging Lululemon (LULU) shares in return for cash for the third time in just over a year.

Wilson was part of a group of investors led by China’s Anta Sports that acquired the company in 2019, ultimately taking the Helsinki-based company public in February 2024.

data.title: Germany's inflation expected to touch 5-month high of 2.2% in July
data.description:
• Germany’s annual consumer price inflation rose to 2.2% in July 2025, up from 2% in June and above market expectations of 2.1%.

  • It was the highest rate since March, after two months at the ECB’s 2% target.

  • The Consumer Price Index in Germany increased 0.10% in August of 2025 over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Cameco sees lower full-year production from McArthur River mine transition delays
data.description:
Cameco (NYSE:CCJ) +2.2% pre-market Friday after disclosing that development delays in transitioning its McArthur River mine in Saskatchewan to new mining areas are expected to defer some production and impact its 2025 production forecast.

As Cameco (NYSE:CCJ) flags output delays, other uranium miners are rising in the pre-market, including Uranium Energy (UEC) +3.8%, NexGen Energy (NXE) +3.6%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) +3.4%, Denison Mines (DNN) +2.7%.

In a 6-K filing, Cameco (NYSE:CCJ) said it now expects production from the McArthur River/Key Lake operation at 14M-15M lbs of uranium concentrate, compared to its previous forecast of 18M lbs, but the deferred production may be partially offset by a strong performance at the Cigar Lake mine in Saskatchewan, potentially offsetting up to 1M lbs of the uranium concentrate shortfall at McArthur River/Key Lake.unable to fully mitigate the expected impact of the delayed development and slower than anticipated ground freezing in the first half of 2025

While Cameco (CCJ) said it is unable to fully mitigate the expected impact of the delayed development and slower than anticipated ground freezing in this year's H1, the company said its "strategy, which aligns our marketing, operational and financial decisions to capture full-cycle value, positions us to effectively manage the expected production shortfall and meet our delivery commitments to our customers."

The McArthur River mine is owned 69.8% by Cameco (CCJ) and 30.25% by Orano, the Key Lake mill is owned 83.33% by Cameco and 16.67% by Orano, and the Cigar Lake operation is owned 54.55% by Cameco, 40.45% by Orano and 5% by TEPCO Resources.

Separately, Cameco (CCJ) and Orano said they signed a 15-year agreement worth ~$500M with Saskatchewan regional airline Rise Air to provide workforce transportation services.

ETFs: (URA), (NLR), (URNM)

data.title: Denmark cuts growth forecast amid troubles for Novo Nordisk
data.description:
Denmark on Friday slashed its 2025 growth forecast to 1.4% from 3% projected in May, citing, among other things, weaker prospects for the country’s largest company, the weight loss drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO).

In addition to economic troubles for the Wegovy maker, Denmark’s economy ministry noted that higher tariffs imposed by the Trump administration on exports from the Scandinavian country also led to the revision.

"The pharmaceutical industry is increasingly challenged by competition in the markets for weight loss products, which has dampened growth expectations in the industry," a statement from the ministry said, according to Reuters.

Danish exports have dropped significantly this year due to market share losses for Novo Nordisk (NVO), the government said, noting that its growth forecast for the country’s exports now stands at 0.9% compared to 4.3% in May.

The country expects its economic growth and exports to recover next year, with its growth projections for GDP and exports at 2.1% and 2.9%, respectively.

“We expect that the pharmaceutical industry will continue to be of great importance to the Danish economy, even though we cannot expect the same extraordinarily large growth as in recent years,” Bloomberg News reported, citing a statement from Danish economy minister Stephanie Lose.

Its revised growth outlook came weeks after Novo (OTCPK:NONOF) lowered its full-year growth outlook for sales and operating profit, citing pressure on its obesity franchise led by its GLP-1 medication semaglutide, marketed as Ozempic and Wegovy.

data.title: Marvell tumbles as Wall Street worries over AI future
data.description:
Marvell Technology's (NASDAQ:MRVL) recent quarterly results and guidance were nothing short of a disappointment to Wall Street, as investors expressed concern over the company's future in the artificial intelligence spending boom. 

Shares sank 13% in premarket trading, adding to the more than 30% decline year-to-date.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said he did “not hear the same level of confidence/visibility” about the company's AI growth prospects in the near and medium term. As such, he downgraded Marvell to Neutral from Buy and cut his price target to $78 from $90.

“At this point, we sense incrementally higher uncertainty around both: the timing of MRVL’s new (Microsoft Maia) project (could be more FY28/CY27E rather than 2HFY27/CY26E); and share in next-gen 3nm Amazon project,” Arya wrote in a note to clients. “As a result, we lower CY26 data center growth to mid-teens YoY from 23-25% YoY prior. In our view, a Neutral rating better reflects/balances MRVL’s opportunity/ breadth of IP with lumpiness in AI sales, customer [centric] risks, and strong competition for custom chip sockets.”

Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton also lowered his price target and said that he too did not hear the same level of enthusiasm about Marvell's prospects, particularly as it related to next-gen chips for Amazon (AMZN) and Microsoft (MSFT).

“While the custom silicon business remains on track to grow in F2H26 vs. F1H26, the business is expected to decline meaningfully in F3Q26 (we model -15% Q/Q) before rebounding strongly in F4Q26,” Bolton wrote in a note to clients. “Timing of customer deliveries, presumably at its largest customer, and changes in the supply chain are driving this lumpiness.”
